title: psdef.txt
text: psdef.txt The psdef.txt file is a configuration file for PostScript printing on Unix platforms. Description You can edit the psdef.txt to override default font mappings or add paper sizes. The file contains other sections as well, but it is recommended that you not modify these. A # character at the beginning of any line in this file denotes a comment. File Sections The file is divided into the following sections: [FONTS] —font mappings. For more information, see Font Support for PostScript Output . [background] and [foreground] —color mappings for printing. [PAPERS] —paper size definitions. [GENERAL] —compression and encoding parameters Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
